{"notes":"iOS Specific Issues Found:

1. WiFiInfoService.percentToApproxDBm() converts signalStrength (0.0-1.0) to dBm, but when signalStrength is 0, it returns nil signalDBm
2. When NEHotspotNetwork.fetchCurrent() fails or returns 0 strength, service returns nil or WiFiInfo with nil signalDBm
3. WiFiMeasurementEngine.buildPoint() then uses  causing the -100dBm default
4. iOS ViewModel doesn't have live RSSI polling (currentRSSI exists but never updates except during measurement)
5. Location permission check happens but no UI feedback when denied

Fix needed:
- Add live RSSI timer to update currentRSSI every 1s (AC-1.4 requirement)
- Improve error handling when WiFi fetch fails
- Add permission request UI when not authorized"}

{"id":"NetMonitor20-p73","title":"Heatmap: Fix WiFi signal always returning -100dBm","description":"MeasurementPoint defaults to -100dBm when WiFiInfo is nil. WiFiMeasurementEngine calls wifiService.fetchCurrentWiFi() but MacWiFiInfoService uses nonisolated(unsafe) pattern that may fail to return data.

Issues to investigate:
1. CoreWLAN may require Location permission on macOS 15+
2. CWWiFiClient.interface() returns nil if WiFi is off or permission denied
3. rssiValue() returns 0 if not associated

{"id":"NetMonitor20-hay","title":"WiFi Heatmap Phase 3 — AR Continuous Scan","description":"Phase 3 implements real-time AR continuous scanning for iOS (LiDAR required). Three concurrent pipelines run simultaneously: AR mesh capture (60fps), Wi-Fi measurement (2Hz adaptive), and Metal-based map rendering (10Hz). Users walk through a space while the app builds a real-time 2D map with heatmap overlay using persistent Metal textures (2048x2048). WiFiman color scheme (blue→cyan→green→yellow→orange→red). Split-screen UI shows AR camera (top 40%) and growing 2D map (bottom 60%). Includes pause/resume with AR relocalization, finish scan with IDW refinement, post-scan visualization switching, and comprehensive performance management (CPU\u003c60%, GPU\u003c40%, memory\u003c600MB, battery\u003c15%/30min). Thermal throttling via ThermalThrottleMonitor integration. P1 features: AP roaming overlay, walking path trace, coverage completeness indicator.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"blake.crane@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T05:15:31Z","created_by":"Blake Crane","updated_at":"2026-03-12T16:41:50Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T16:41:50Z","close_reason":"Deferred: AR Continuous Scan epic deferred to post-Phase 1."}

{"id":"NetMonitor20-x47","title":"WiFi Heatmap Phase 2 — AR Map Creation","description":"Phase 2 adds AR-assisted map creation and survey for iOS. Users scan their room with the device camera (LiDAR preferred, non-LiDAR supported with reduced accuracy). ARKit mesh data is processed through a pipeline (height filter → XZ projection → rasterization → Gaussian blur → contour detection) to generate a floor plan automatically. After scanning, the app transitions to Phase 1 survey mode with the AR-generated floor plan pre-loaded and pre-calibrated. AR position tracking enables auto-placement of measurement points. Includes tracking loss fallback to manual tap mode, floor plan editing (P1), and mesh classification for doors/windows (P1). iOS only — requires ARKit support.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"blake.crane@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T05:15:20Z","created_by":"Blake Crane","updated_at":"2026-03-12T16:41:50Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T16:41:50Z","close_reason":"Deferred: AR Map Creation epic deferred to post-Phase 1."}

{"id":"NetMonitor20-q9a","title":"WiFi Heatmap Phase 1 — Blueprint Walk Survey","description":"Phase 1 implements the core blueprint-based Wi-Fi heatmap survey for both macOS and iOS. Users import a floor plan image, optionally calibrate scale with a two-point measurement, then walk the space tapping/clicking to collect Wi-Fi measurements at each location. The app renders IDW-interpolated heatmap overlays showing signal strength, SNR, speed, and latency. Includes project save/load (.netmonsurvey format), PDF export (macOS), measurement management (inspect, delete, undo), and live RSSI monitoring. macOS: full 5-visualization-type experience with CoreWLAN data. iOS: 3 visualization types with liquid glass styling and NEHotspotNetwork data.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"blake.crane@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T05:15:11Z","created_by":"Blake Crane","updated_at":"2026-03-12T16:41:58Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T16:41:58Z","close_reason":"Completed: All macOS tasks done. iOS tasks deferred.
